translation:
Registering property under another person's name is by no means a "Debt-Proof Safe"! Creditors need only focus on the three core elements—source of funding, actual control, and the timeline of debts—and, combined with the flexible application of legal tools, can negate the effect of the principle of external appearance of registration through legal proceedings, leaving hidden assets nowhere to hide!

(The parties in the cases mentioned in the text are pseudonyms. For individual case consultation, please contact a professional enforcement attorney.)

— Focused on the enforcement field for 10 years, we make judgments resound on the ground —

Reference Materials:

*Civil Code of the People's Republic of China*

Article 154: A civil juristic act conducted by an actor and the counterparty with malicious collusion that harms the lawful rights and interests of others is void.

Article 303: Where co-owners have agreed not to partition the jointly owned immovable or movable property in order to maintain the co-ownership relationship, such agreement shall be followed; however, if a co-owner has a major reason for partition, partition may be requested. If there is no agreement or the agreement is unclear, a co-owner by shares may request partition at any time; a joint co-owner may request partition when the basis of joint ownership ceases to exist or when there is a major reason for partition. If partition causes damage to other co-owners, compensation shall be made.

Article 538: Where a debtor disposes of property rights gratuitously in any of the following ways, affecting the realization of the creditor's claim, the creditor may request the people's court to revoke the debtor's actions: (1) waiving claims; (2) waiving security for claims; (3) transferring property without consideration; or (4) maliciously extending the performance period of due claims.

Article 541: The right of revocation shall be exercised within one year from the date on which the creditor becomes aware or should have become aware of the grounds for revocation. If the right of revocation is not exercised within five years from the date of the debtor's action, it shall be extinguished.

*Criminal Law of the People's Republic of China*

Article 313: [Crime of Refusing to Execute a Judgment or Ruling] Where a person who has the ability to execute a judgment or ruling of a people's court refuses to do so, and the circumstances are serious, he shall be sentenced to fixed-term imprisonment of not more than three years, criminal detention, or a fine; where the circumstances are especially serious, he shall be sentenced to fixed-term imprisonment of not less than three years but not more than seven years, and a fine.

*Provisions of the Supreme People's Court on Sealing, Seizing, and Freezing Property in Civil Enforcement by People's Courts*

Article 12: Where the property is jointly owned by the enforcement debtor and other persons, the people's court may seal, seize, or freeze such property and promptly notify the co-owners.

Where the co-owners agree to partition the jointly owned property and the creditor recognizes such partition, the people's court may deem it valid. The effect of the seal, seizure, or freezing extends to the property within the share of the enforcement debtor after the partition agreement; the people's court shall issue an order to lift the seal, seizure, or freezing on the property within the shares of other co-owners.

Where a co-owner initiates a partition action, or the applying executor initiates a subrogation partition action, the people's court shall permit it. During the litigation, the enforcement of the property shall be suspended.

Article 13, Paragraphs 1 and 2: Where a third party holds the property of the enforcement debtor for the benefit of the enforcement debtor, the people's court may seal, seize, or freeze such property; where the property is designated to be kept by the third party continuously, the third party shall not deliver it to the enforcement debtor.

Where a third party lawfully holds the property of the enforcement debtor for his own benefit, the people's court may seal, seize, or freeze such property; the third party may continue to possess and use the property but shall not deliver it to the enforcement debtor.

(2020) Supreme People's Court Civil Petition No. 6800
(2020) Supreme People's Court Civil Petition No. 5648
(2021) Yu 05 Civil Final No. 888
(2022) Xiang 0725 Civil First Instance No. 3288
(2023) Yu 0231 Civil First Instance No. 2731
(2024) Yun 01 Civil Final No. 959
(2025) Xin 40 Criminal Final No. 9
